page_summary: Page 1: Assembly instructions for a 3-seat sofa with safety advice and a parts list. Page 2: Parts List with screws, washers, nuts, and tools for assembly. Page 3: Page shows steps 3–5 of assembling a curved cabinet/assembly, with diagrams of screw hardware and a note advising not to tighten screws until the full piece is assembled. Page 4: This page contains Step 6 and Step 7 of sofa assembly, with a general tip not to tighten screws until the whole piece is assembled.

instruction_steps.text: The best advice is not tightening up any screws until the whole piece is assembled.
The best advice is not tightening up any screws until the whole piece is assembled.
When finish all the assembly of the sofa, adjust the middle legs and make the sofa parallel to the ground.

inventory.inferred_function: Fasten and join components during final assembly.
inventory.function_rationale: The instruction advises not to tighten any screws until the entire piece is assembled, indicating screws are used to secure parts together at the end of the build.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: Small M6 screw with hex head and internal drive for final fastening.
inventory.shape: Cylindrical screw with a hexagonal head
inventory.size_relative: Small relative to larger kit fasteners; likely 6 mm diameter and 15 mm length
inventory.thickness_or_depth: Moderate diameter with a long threaded shaft (length around 15 mm)
inventory.edge_profile: Threaded portion along the shaft; head region smooth to slightly profiled; no visible under-head washer in close-up
inventory.surface_texture: Fine threads along shaft; hexagonal head surface
inventory.symmetry: Axially symmetric about the screw axis
orientation_cues: Hexagonal head orientation identifies drive face; flat surfaces align with driving tool
Axis runs straight through center from head to tip
distinctive_landmarks: Internal drive socket recess in head

differences: Often similar; but may differ in head shape or drive type; this part explicitly has a hex head with internal hex socket and is sized as M6*15, the smallest shown in kit
Bolt typically has a larger head and requires nut; this entry implies a via-threaded hole without a nut, and has a drive recess suitable for a hex key or Allen wrench

Self-tapping screws cut their own threads into material; here the observed shaft appears to have standard machine-threaded features and is used in assembly with pre-threaded or tapped holes in final steps
This is a specific fastener with M6 thread and 15 mm length and a hex head with internal drive, not a general or unspecified fastener

action_relevant_notes: Use a hex key/Allen wrench or appropriate driver compatible with the internal socket to drive the screw.
Insert into mating threaded hole after other components are in place; do not tighten until full assembly is ready.
Feel for the defined hex-head surfaces to orient the screw prior to driving; avoid cross-threading by aligning with the hole before turning.
uncertainties: Exact length (15 mm) confirmed by label but not measured in image
Whether shaft has full thread along entire length or partial (thread length not fully visible)
Presence of a washer under head not shown in close-up

inventory.inferred_function: Fastens or secures components together during assembly; provides removable fastening for modular parts.

The instruction advises delaying tightening until the entire piece is assembled, which is typical for screws used to connect multiple components in a reversible manner.

inventory.inferred_function: Distributes load of M6 screws and provides a stable seating surface; acts as a spacer/shim to protect joined surfaces and help maintain joint integrity.
inventory.function_rationale: The instruction about not tightening screws until the whole piece is assembled implies washers are placed on screws during assembly to distribute clamping force and protect surfaces. The designation M6 indicates a standard metric washer size used with M6 screws.

inventory.inferred_function: Provides a threaded receptacle to mate with an M6 bolt, enabling parts to be securely fastened together in an assembled structure.
inventory.function_rationale: The label indicates a metric M6 nut. Nuts are used with bolts to create a clamp that holds parts together; the general assembly guidance about delaying tightening until the entire piece is assembled supports its role in final fastening of a multi-part assembly.
inventory.one_sentence_summary: A small hexagonal nut with a through-hole that accepts an M6 bolt to fasten parts together.

size and thread pitch differ; outer hex size or perceived mass/volume varies; through-hole diameter corresponds to M5 or M8 thread, not M6

action_relevant_notes: To identify by touch: feel for the six flat sides of the hex shape; locate a smooth, circular through-hole in the center; confirm the outer faces are flat and untextured along edges (metal nut standard)
When using: align nut with bolt by inserting bolt through hole; rotate nut to engage threads
Handle with care to avoid dropping; the hex shape helps manual turning
If uncertain about size: compare to known M6 bolt by rough diameter of bore and hex face size

inventory.inferred_function: A tool used to tighten or loosen hex socket screws/bolts during assembly.

Step 5 only mentions S-J, implying X6 remains part of the preceding assembly and is not replaced by S-J, reinforcing its role as a structural/connector element rather than a one-time fastener.

nails have minimal or no threaded shaft and are driven with a hammer; screws have a distinct threaded portion and a visible head designed for a screw driver
rivets are usually permanent fasteners without a removable nut and not designed for re-tightening after assembly
studs are long, frequently threaded pins not intended as removable fasteners; screws shown here are shorter and designed to be driven into or through components with a driver

action_relevant_notes: In assembly, identify the screw by a circular head (if visible) and threaded shaft; use a screwdriver to drive and tighten after alignment
Remember that tightening is done after the overall structure is aligned
